moves.note: Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero

plan: Trim SPY to cure concentration and meet the single-name mandate.
Add to QQQI to favor income and achieve more stable returns.
Sell QQQ, SMH, and SOXL to exit flagged positions, reduce tech concentration, and eliminate all leveraged ETFs.
Trim CRWD to reduce single-name concentration.
Buy OXY for diversification and to add a strong scanner setup.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets capital preservation, aiming for ~1.5% per month. It significantly de-risks the portfolio by cutting leveraged ETFs, reducing concentration, and favoring income/broad index. Expected drawdown is lower, but growth potential is also limited.

plan: Trim SPY to cure concentration and meet the single-name mandate.
Trim QQQI to balance income with growth objectives.
Sell QQQ, SMH, and SOXL to exit all flagged positions and reduce overall leverage.
Buy MSFU and OXY to add top-scoring scanner setups and diversify the portfolio.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets risk-first growth, aiming for ~2.5% per month. It reduces leverage and concentration while blending broad index exposure with quality tech and diversification. It offers a moderate risk/reward profile.

plan: Trim SPY to cure concentration and meet the single-name mandate.
Sell QQQI to shift focus from income towards aggressive growth.
Sell QQQ, SMH, and SOXL to exit all flagged positions.
Buy MSFU, concentrating on a top-scoring scanner setup.
Buy SOXS to add a high-reward inverse semiconductor hedge.
aggressive.read: This aggressive growth plan targets ~4% per month, leaning into leverage and concentration. It carries significantly higher risk, with potential for larger drawdowns and daily decay from leveraged ETFs. Strict stop discipline is crucial.

moves.note: Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero

plan: Trim SPY to cure concentration and meet the single-name mandate.
Add significantly to QQQI to prioritize income and steady distributions.
Sell QQQ, CRWD, SMH, and SOXL to exit flagged positions, reduce high-beta exposure, and eliminate all leveraged ETFs.
Buy OXY for diversification into a more stable sector.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1% per month, prioritizing low drawdown and reliable distributions. While aiming for steady cash-like income, distributions are variable and NAV can still fall in market selloffs. Growth potential is minimal.
